items.title: USDA tracker: SNAP food-restriction waivers continue rolling out through 2026
items.summary: USDA's official tracker lists approved state SNAP food-restriction waivers with 2026 effective dates including Florida (April 20), Colorado (April 30), Arkansas (July 1), and Montana (September 30). Waivers vary by state but generally restrict purchases of sodas, candy, and certain prepared foods.

items.title: USDA finalizes SNAP retailer stocking standards; compliance required by Nov. 4, 2026
items.summary: USDA's final rule requires most SNAP-authorized retailers to stock at least 7 varieties in each of 4 staple food categories (fruits/vegetables, meat/poultry/fish, dairy products, breads/cereals). Compliance is required by November 4, 2026.

items.title: CMS guidance: federal Medicaid/CHIP matching limits for certain noncitizens take effect Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued implementation guidance on new statutory limits to federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for individuals who are not U.S. citizens or nationals, effective October 1, 2026. Exceptions include emergency medical conditions and lawfully residing children/pregnant people under existing state options.

items.title: HUD: HOTMA Sections 102/104 enforcement set for Jan. 1, 2027; prep starts Sept. 2026
items.summary: HUD set January 1, 2027 as the enforcement date for HOTMA Sections 102 (income calculation) and 104 (asset limits) for most PHAs. PHAs need to begin practical implementation prep around September 2026 to update software, policies, and tenant notices.

items.title: SSA: Direct Express transitions to Fifth Third Bank; new enrollments begin May 2026
items.summary: Social Security Direct Express prepaid debit card services are transitioning to Fifth Third Bank as the new financial agent. New enrollments under Fifth Third begin in May 2026; existing cardholders will receive instructions for the transition.

items.title: SSA 2026 COLA at 2.8%; SSI max is $994/month (individual), $1,491 (couple)
items.summary: SSA confirmed a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for 2026 affecting about 75 million Americans. The federal SSI payment standard is $994/month for individuals and $1,491/month for couples. Disability work thresholds are $1,690/month (non-blind SGA) and $2,830/month (blind SGA).

items.title: USDA publishes 2026-27 WIC income eligibility guidelines; effective July 1, 2026
items.summary: USDA published the 2026-27 WIC income eligibility guidelines, effective July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027. States may set income limits up to 185% of the federal poverty level. Participants enrolled in SNAP, TANF, or Medicaid automatically meet WIC income eligibility.

items.title: IRS: 2025 EITC max $8,046; CTC up to $2,200 with refundable ACTC up to $1,700
items.summary: For tax year 2025, the maximum EITC is $8,046 (3+ qualifying children). The Child Tax Credit is up to $2,200 per qualifying child, with up to $1,700 refundable via the Additional Child Tax Credit. The CTC requires at least $2,500 in earned income to begin phasing in the refundable portion.

items.title: Taxpayer Advocate: July 10, 2026 deadline for COVID-era penalty refund/protective claims
items.summary: The Taxpayer Advocate Service is warning taxpayers about a July 10, 2026 deadline to file certain COVID-era penalty and interest refund claims or protective claims with the IRS. Taxpayers who believe they qualify should consult a tax professional or call TAS for free assistance.
